source.description: A six-panel raster marker contact sheet generated for the locked July 8 Doodlea.day slug, then cropped into step frames and the finished master.
source.notes: The sheet must keep one consistent alarm clock pose with no face, text, labels, arrows, or sticker/badge border.

process_strategy: The alarm clock is built from the body circle, bells, feet, hands, tick marks, color blocks, and shadow before the final only thickens outlines and evens marker fills.

final_elements.name: round clock body
final_elements.description: A large round clock face and outer body.

final_elements.name: twin alarm bells
final_elements.description: Two small rounded bells on top with a simple bridge.

final_elements.name: clock feet and handle
final_elements.description: Tiny feet below the body and a curved handle between the bells.

final_elements.name: hands and tick marks
final_elements.description: Two clock hands and a few chunky tick marks, with no numbers or words.

final_elements.name: blue body fill
final_elements.description: Bright blue marker fill on the clock body.

final_elements.name: yellow bells and red hands
final_elements.description: Yellow marker fill on the bells and red or orange marker on the hands.

final_elements.name: purple ground shadow
final_elements.description: A small marker shadow under the feet.

frames.visible_job: Blocks the large round clock body with a simple inner face circle.
Adds twin alarm bells on top while keeping the body circle unchanged.
Adds small feet and a top handle to the existing clock body.
Adds the two hands and chunky tick marks inside the existing face.
Adds blue body fill, yellow bell fill, red hands, and a purple ground shadow.

final_step.visible_job: Only thickens the existing black outlines, evens marker fills, clarifies the hands, ticks, bells, feet, and shadow.
allowed_changes: small highlight gaps on existing color areas

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
